101		ModelRef      string // catalog model-ref; passed through to agent as -model-ref
102		Effort        string
103		ContextBudget string // prompt budget: "", "minimal" (omits large governing docs for cheap-tier)
104		PromptFile    string // override prompt file (auto-generated if empty)
105		WorkerID      string // from DDX_WORKER_ID env or caller
106		// BeadEvents, when non-nil, receives a kind:routing evidence entry after
107		// the agent run completes. This is the hook that feeds the cost-tiered
108		// routing analytics described in FEAT-routing-visibility.
109		BeadEvents BeadEventAppender
110		// MirrorCfg, when non-nil and configured, enables mirroring the
111		// finalized .ddx/executions/<attempt>/ bundle to an out-of-band archive
112		// after the worker writes the result. Failures never affect the bead
113		// outcome — see executions_mirror.go.
114		MirrorCfg *config.ExecutionsMirrorConfig
115		// Service, when non-nil, is the agentlib.DdxAgent used to dispatch the
116		// agent invocation. Production callers leave this nil — ExecuteBead
117		// constructs a fresh service from projectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir.

121		// the agent invocation. Production callers leave this nil. Tests use this
122		// seam to return canned *Result values without spinning up a real service
123		// or provider chain. When set, it takes precedence over Service.
124		AgentRunner AgentRunner

144	// ExecuteBeadWithConfig is the SD-024 successor to ExecuteBead. It
145	// accepts a sealed ResolvedConfig (durable knobs) and an
146	// ExecuteBeadRuntime (plumbing + per-invocation intent), assembles an
147	// equivalent ExecuteBeadOptions, and delegates to ExecuteBead.
148	// Behavior is identical to ExecuteBead with an equivalently-populated
149	// ExecuteBeadOptions.
150	//
151	// Stage 3 of SD-024: this function exists alongside ExecuteBead;
152	// production callers have not migrated yet.
153	func ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, beadID string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadRuntime, gitOps GitOps) (*ExecuteBeadResult, error) {
154		opts := ExecuteBeadOptions{
155			FromRev:       runtime.FromRev,
156			Harness:       rcfg.Harness(),
157			Model:         rcfg.Model(),
158			Provider:      rcfg.Provider(),
159			ModelRef:      rcfg.ModelRef(),
160			Effort:        rcfg.Effort(),
161			ContextBudget: rcfg.ContextBudget(),
162			PromptFile:    runtime.PromptFile,
163			WorkerID:      runtime.WorkerID,
164			BeadEvents:    runtime.BeadEvents,
165			MirrorCfg:     rcfg.MirrorConfig(),
166			Service:       runtime.Service,
167			AgentRunner:   runtime.AgentRunner,
168		}
169		return ExecuteBead(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, opts, gitOps)
170	}

172	// GitOps abstracts the git operations required by the worker.
173	// Merge is intentionally excluded — that belongs to the parent-side
174	// orchestrator (OrchestratorGitOps). UpdateRef/DeleteRef are exposed here
175	// so landing-side helpers (e.g. BuildLandingGateContext) can pin a
176	// transient ref while running gate evaluation against an ephemeral
177	// worktree.
178	type GitOps interface {
179		HeadRev(dir string) (string, error)
180		ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error)
181		WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error
182		WorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath string) error
183		WorktreeList(dir string) ([]string, error)
184		WorktreePrune(dir string) error
185		IsDirty(dir string) (bool, error)
186		// SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes (excluding harness noise paths) and
187		// commits them using msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil) when a
188		// commit was made, (false, nil) when there was nothing real to commit (all
189		// dirty files were noise), and (false, err) on failure.
190		SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error)
191		// UpdateRef updates ref in dir to sha. Used by landing helpers to pin a
192		// commit so a transient worktree can check it out without racing with
193		// other work that might prune it.
194		UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error
195		// DeleteRef removes ref from dir. Used to unpin a transient ref after
196		// the consumer (e.g. an ephemeral worktree) is done with it.
197		DeleteRef(dir, ref string) error
198	}

589	// ExecuteBead is the thin worker: it creates an isolated worktree, constructs
590	// the agent prompt from bead context, runs the agent harness, synthesizes a
591	// commit if the agent left uncommitted changes, then cleans up the worktree
592	// and returns the result. It classifies outcomes as exactly one of:
593	//
594	//   - task_succeeded: agent exited 0 and produced one or more commits
595	//   - task_failed:    agent exited non-zero
596	//   - task_no_changes: agent exited 0 but made no commits
597	//
598	// Merge, UpdateRef, gate evaluation, preserve-ref management, and orphan
599	// recovery are the parent's responsibility (see LandBeadResult, RecoverOrphans).
600	//
601	// Agent dispatch: production callers leave opts.Service and opts.AgentRunner
602	// nil. ExecuteBead constructs a fresh agentlib.DdxAgent from projectRoot via
603	// NewServiceFromWorkDir and dispatches via RunViaServiceWith. Tests may set
604	// opts.AgentRunner to inject a fake that returns canned Result values; when
605	// set, it takes precedence over the service path.
606	func ExecuteBead(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, beadID string, opts ExecuteBeadOptions, gitOps GitOps) (*ExecuteBeadResult, error) {
607		if ctx == nil {
608			ctx = context.Background()
609		}
610		attemptID := GenerateAttemptID()
611		if opts.WorkerID == "" {
612			opts.WorkerID = os.Getenv("DDX_WORKER_ID")
613		}
614	
615		wtPath := executeBeadWorktreePath(beadID, attemptID)
616		if mkErr := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(wtPath), 0o755); mkErr != nil {
617			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating execute-bead worktree parent dir: %w", mkErr)
618		}
619	
620		// Commit beads.jsonl before spawning worktree so the worktree snapshot
621		// includes any bead metadata updates (e.g. spec-id).
622		if err := CommitTracker(projectRoot); err != nil {
623			return nil, err
624		}
625	
626		// Checkpoint any remaining caller dirt as a real commit on the current
627		// branch (FEAT-012 §22, US-126 AC#1). The new HEAD becomes the effective
628		// base for the worker worktree; caller's edits are preserved as a normal
629		// commit they can `git reset HEAD~` if they want them back uncommitted.
630		if _, err := gitOps.SynthesizeCommit(projectRoot, "chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead "+attemptID); err != nil {
631			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pre-execute-bead checkpoint: %w", err)
632		}
633	
634		// Resolve base revision after the tracker + checkpoint commits.
635		baseRev, err := resolveBase(gitOps, projectRoot, opts.FromRev)
636		if err != nil {
637			return nil, err
638		}
639	
640		// Create the isolated worktree. Orphan recovery is the parent's responsibility
641		// (call RecoverOrphans before invoking workers).
642		if err := gitOps.WorktreeAdd(projectRoot, wtPath, baseRev); err != nil {
643			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating isolated worktree: %w", err)
644		}
645		defer func() {
646			_ = gitOps.WorktreeRemove(projectRoot, wtPath)
647		}()
648	
649		// Publish the live run-state so operators and HELIX can observe what is
650		// executing without polling the bead tracker (CONTRACT-001 §5). The file
651		// is removed on completion; a crashed worker leaves a stale file that
652		// RecoverOrphans sweeps before the next attempt.
653		_ = WriteRunState(projectRoot, RunState{
654			BeadID:       beadID,
655			AttemptID:    attemptID,
656			Harness:      opts.Harness,
657			Model:        opts.Model,
658			StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
659			WorktreePath: wtPath,
660		})
661		defer func() {
662			_ = ClearRunState(projectRoot)
663		}()
664	
665		// Repair project-local skill symlinks whose targets do not resolve inside
666		// the freshly created worktree.
667		_ = materializeWorktreeSkills(wtPath)
668	
669		// Prepare artifacts (context load, prompt generation).
670		artifacts, err := prepareArtifacts(projectRoot, wtPath, beadID, attemptID, baseRev, opts)
671		if err != nil {
672			res := &ExecuteBeadResult{
673				BeadID:    beadID,
674				AttemptID: attemptID,
675				WorkerID:  opts.WorkerID,
676				BaseRev:   baseRev,
677				ResultRev: baseRev, // no commits; ResultRev == BaseRev signals no output
678				ExitCode:  1,
679				Error:     err.Error(),
680				Outcome:   ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed,
681			}
682			if abInfo, _ := os.Stat(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID)); abInfo != nil && abInfo.IsDir() {
683				res.ExecutionDir = filepath.Join(ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID)
684			}
685			res.FailureMode = ClassifyFailureMode(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Error)
686			populateWorkerStatus(res)
687			_ = writeArtifactJSON(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID, "result.json"), res)
688			return res, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead context load: %w", err)
689		}
690	
691		// Pre-create the execution bundle dir in the worktree so the agent can write
692		// artifacts (e.g. no_changes_rationale.txt) without needing to create the
693		// directory itself. Failures are non-fatal: the agent can create it on demand.
694		_ = os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(wtPath, artifacts.DirRel), 0o755)
695	
696		// Redirect per-run session/telemetry output into the DDx-owned execution
697		// bundle so the embedded harness does not accumulate state at the worktree root.
698		embeddedStateDir := filepath.Join(artifacts.DirAbs, "embedded")
699		if err := os.MkdirAll(embeddedStateDir, 0o755); err != nil {
700			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating embedded state dir: %w", err)
701		}
702	
703		sessionID := GenerateSessionID()
704		startedAt := time.Now().UTC()
705	
706		runOpts := RunOptions{
707			Context:       ctx,
708			Harness:       opts.Harness,
709			Prompt:        "",
710			PromptFile:    artifacts.PromptAbs,
711			Model:         opts.Model,
712			Provider:      opts.Provider,
713			ModelRef:      opts.ModelRef,
714			Effort:        opts.Effort,
715			WorkDir:       wtPath,
716			Permissions:   "unrestricted", // isolated worktree; writes must not require approval
717			SessionLogDir: embeddedStateDir,
718			Correlation: map[string]string{
719				"bead_id":     beadID,
720				"base_rev":    baseRev,
721				"attempt_id":  attemptID,
722				"session_id":  sessionID,
723				"worker_id":   opts.WorkerID,
724				"bundle_path": artifacts.DirRel,
725				"prompt_file": artifacts.PromptRel,
726			},
727		}
728	
729		agentResult, agentErr := dispatchAgentRun(ctx, projectRoot, opts, runOpts)
730		finishedAt := time.Now().UTC()
731	
732		exitCode := 0
733		tokens := 0
734		inputTokens := 0
735		outputTokens := 0
736		costUSD := 0.0
737		resultModel := opts.Model
738		resultHarness := opts.Harness
739		resultProvider := ""
740		agentErrMsg := ""
741		if agentResult != nil {
742			exitCode = agentResult.ExitCode
743			tokens = agentResult.Tokens
744			inputTokens = agentResult.InputTokens
745			outputTokens = agentResult.OutputTokens
746			costUSD = agentResult.CostUSD
747			if agentResult.Error != "" {
748				agentErrMsg = agentResult.Error
749			}
750			if agentResult.Model != "" {
751				resultModel = agentResult.Model
752			}
753			if agentResult.Provider != "" {
754				resultProvider = agentResult.Provider
755			}
756			if agentResult.Harness != "" {
757				resultHarness = agentResult.Harness
758			}
759		}
760		if agentErr != nil {
761			if exitCode == 0 {
762				exitCode = 1
763			}
764			agentErrMsg = agentErr.Error()
765		}
766	
767		// Capture routing evidence from the agent result. These fields are
768		// populated by RunAgent (embedded harness) and RunScript (script harness).
769		routeReason := ""
770		routeBaseURL := ""
771		if agentResult != nil {
772			routeReason = agentResult.RouteReason
773			routeBaseURL = agentResult.ResolvedBaseURL
774		}
775	
776		// Get the HEAD of the worktree after the agent ran.
777		resultRev, revErr := gitOps.HeadRev(wtPath)
778		if revErr != nil {
779			res := &ExecuteBeadResult{
780				BeadID:       beadID,
781				AttemptID:    attemptID,
782				WorkerID:     opts.WorkerID,
783				BaseRev:      baseRev,
784				ResultRev:    baseRev, // no commits readable; treat as no output
785				Harness:      resultHarness,
786				Provider:     resultProvider,
787				Model:        resultModel,
788				SessionID:    sessionID,
789				DurationMS:   int(finishedAt.Sub(startedAt).Milliseconds()),
790				Tokens:       tokens,
791				CostUSD:      costUSD,
792				ExitCode:     1,
793				Error:        agentErrMsg,
794				Reason:       revErr.Error(), // HeadRev failure; orchestrator prefers this over Error for Reason
795				ExecutionDir: artifacts.DirRel,
796				PromptFile:   artifacts.PromptRel,
797				ManifestFile: artifacts.ManifestRel,
798				ResultFile:   artifacts.ResultRel,
799				StartedAt:    startedAt,
800				FinishedAt:   finishedAt,
801				Outcome:      ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed,
802			}
803			res.FailureMode = ClassifyFailureMode(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Error)
804			populateWorkerStatus(res)
805			_ = writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ResultAbs, res)
806			return res, fmt.Errorf("failed to read worktree HEAD: %w", revErr)
807		}
808	
809		// Write usage.json when the harness reports token usage or cost.
810		// Done before SynthesizeCommit so usage data is available in the
811		// preliminary result written for commit-message sourcing.
812		var usageFileRel string
813		if tokens > 0 || costUSD > 0 {
814			usage := executeBeadUsage{
815				AttemptID:    attemptID,
816				Harness:      resultHarness,
817				Provider:     resultProvider,
818				Model:        resultModel,
819				Tokens:       tokens,
820				InputTokens:  inputTokens,
821				OutputTokens: outputTokens,
822				CostUSD:      costUSD,
823			}
824			if writeErr := writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.UsageAbs, usage); writeErr == nil {
825				usageFileRel = artifacts.UsageRel
826			}
827		}
828	
829		// Synthesize a commit when the agent left tracked edits without committing.
830		// Only do so for real changes — harness noise paths are excluded. If nothing
831		// real was staged (committed is false), leave resultRev == baseRev so the
832		// outcome is classified as task_no_changes.
833		if resultRev == baseRev {
834			if isDirty, _ := gitOps.IsDirty(wtPath); isDirty {
835				// Build a preliminary result and write it to result.json before
836				// calling SynthesizeCommit. The commit message is then sourced from
837				// the tracked artifact file, satisfying the provenance contract:
838				// "commit-message metadata must be projected from tracked artifact
839				// files, never from ad hoc runtime state" (FEAT-006).
840				// The final result.json is re-written after the commit with the
841				// correct ResultRev, Outcome, and Status.
842				prelimOutcome := ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded
843				if exitCode != 0 {
844					prelimOutcome = ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed
845				}
846				prelimRes := &ExecuteBeadResult{
847					BeadID:       beadID,
848					AttemptID:    attemptID,
849					WorkerID:     opts.WorkerID,
850					BaseRev:      baseRev,
851					ResultRev:    "", // unknown until commit is made
852					Harness:      resultHarness,
853					Provider:     resultProvider,
854					Model:        resultModel,
855					SessionID:    sessionID,
856					DurationMS:   int(finishedAt.Sub(startedAt).Milliseconds()),
857					Tokens:       tokens,
858					CostUSD:      costUSD,
859					ExitCode:     exitCode,
860					Error:        agentErrMsg,
861					ExecutionDir: artifacts.DirRel,
862					PromptFile:   artifacts.PromptRel,
863					ManifestFile: artifacts.ManifestRel,
864					ResultFile:   artifacts.ResultRel,
865					UsageFile:    usageFileRel,
866					StartedAt:    startedAt,
867					FinishedAt:   finishedAt,
868					Outcome:      prelimOutcome,
869				}
870				populateWorkerStatus(prelimRes)
871				_ = writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ResultAbs, prelimRes)
872	
873				// Render the commit message from the tracked artifact file.
874				commitMsg, msgErr := BuildCommitMessageFromResultFile(artifacts.ResultAbs)
875				if msgErr != nil {
876					commitMsg = "chore: execute-bead iteration " + beadID
877				}
878	
879				if committed, synthErr := gitOps.SynthesizeCommit(wtPath, commitMsg); synthErr == nil && committed {
880					if newRev, _ := gitOps.HeadRev(wtPath); newRev != baseRev {
881						resultRev = newRev
882					}
883				}
884			}
885		}
886	
887		res := &ExecuteBeadResult{
888			BeadID:       beadID,
889			AttemptID:    attemptID,
890			WorkerID:     opts.WorkerID,
891			BaseRev:      baseRev,
892			ResultRev:    resultRev,
893			Harness:      resultHarness,
894			Provider:     resultProvider,
895			Model:        resultModel,
896			SessionID:    sessionID,
897			DurationMS:   int(finishedAt.Sub(startedAt).Milliseconds()),
898			Tokens:       tokens,
899			CostUSD:      costUSD,
900			ExitCode:     exitCode,
901			Error:        agentErrMsg,
902			ExecutionDir: artifacts.DirRel,
903			PromptFile:   artifacts.PromptRel,
904			ManifestFile: artifacts.ManifestRel,
905			ResultFile:   artifacts.ResultRel,
906			UsageFile:    usageFileRel,
907			StartedAt:    startedAt,
908			FinishedAt:   finishedAt,
909		}
910	
911		// Classify worker outcome: task_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes.
912		// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult + ApplyLandingToResult) will
913		// overwrite Outcome and Status with the landing decision before output.
914		switch {
915		case exitCode != 0:
916			res.Outcome = ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed
917		case resultRev == baseRev:
918			res.Outcome = ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges
919		default:
920			res.Outcome = ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded
921		}
922	
923		// Classify failure mode from worker-level signals. ApplyLandingToResult
924		// may refine this with landing-level signals (merge conflict, gate
925		// failure) before the final result is output.
926		res.FailureMode = ClassifyFailureMode(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Error)
927	
928		// When the outcome is no_changes, attempt to read the agent's rationale file.
929		// The agent is instructed to write this file (relative to the worktree) when it
930		// determines the bead's work is already present. We read it before the deferred
931		// worktree cleanup removes the file.
932		if res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges {

254	// DefaultProfileLadders returns the built-in profile escalation policy.
255	func DefaultProfileLadders() map[string][]string {
256		out := make(map[string][]string, len(defaultProfileLadders))
257		for profile, ladder := range defaultProfileLadders {
258			out[profile] = append([]string(nil), ladder...)
259		}
260		return out
261	}

263	// ResolvedLadder returns the escalation ladder for the named profile. If
264	// ProfileLadders contains an entry for profile, that wins. Otherwise falls
265	// back to the deprecated ProfilePriority for the default profile only. If
266	// neither is set, returns the shipped FEAT-006 profile ladder.
267	func (r *RoutingConfig) ResolvedLadder(profile string) []string {
268		if profile == "" {
269			profile = "default"
270		}
271		if r == nil {
272			if ladder, ok := defaultProfileLadders[profile]; ok {
273				return append([]string(nil), ladder...)
274			}
275			return append([]string(nil), defaultProfileLadders["default"]...)
276		}
277		if r.ProfileLadders != nil {
278			if ladder, ok := r.ProfileLadders[profile]; ok && len(ladder) > 0 {
279				return append([]string(nil), ladder...)
280			}
281		}
282		if profile == "default" && len(r.ProfilePriority) > 0 {
283			return append([]string(nil), r.ProfilePriority...)
284		}
285		if ladder, ok := defaultProfileLadders[profile]; ok {
286			return append([]string(nil), ladder...)
287		}
288		return append([]string(nil), defaultProfileLadders["default"]...)
289	}
